title: Record: 11L, int6+zstd, decoupled WD (val_bpb = 1.1472)
body:
## Summary

Two submissions for the `10min_16mb` track, both using an 11-layer GPT with GQA (NUM_KV_HEADS=4), int6 per-row quantization, fp16 embeddings, zstd-22 compression, and sliding window evaluation (stride=64).

### 1. Main SOTA Record: 11L WD0.038 Non-Val (`2026-03-20_11L_WD038_NonVal`)

| Seed | val_loss | val_bpb | Steps |
|------|----------|---------|-------|
| 1337 | 1.93869 | 1.14821 | 6986 |
| 42 | 1.93202 | 1.14425 | 8202 |
| 7 | 1.94044 | 1.14924 | 6961 |
| **Mean** | **1.93705** | **1.14723** | |

- **Improvement over current SOTA** (notapplica, 1.1748): **0.0275 BPB**
- **p-value**: 0.0008 (Welch's t-test, one-sided) — well below 0.01 threshold
- All artifacts under 16MB (max: 15,905,331 bytes)
- Trained on standard training shards only (no val data)
- Key innovations: decoupled weight decay (0.038) on Muon optimizer reduces quantization gap, enabling 11 layers to fit under 16MB with int6+zstd; higher LR (0.025) improves convergence

### 2. Non-Record Aside: 11L WD0.034 Val-Only (`2026-03-20_11L_WD034_ValOnly`)

| Seed | val_loss | val_bpb | Steps |
|------|----------|---------|-------|
| 1337 | 1.56344 | 0.92596 | 8290 |
| 42 | 1.56780 | 0.92854 | 8252 |
| 7 | 1.56622 | 0.92761 | 8298 |
| **Mean** | **1.56582** | **0.92737** | |

- Trains on `fineweb_val_*.bin` — same architecture with WD=0.034
- Included as a **non-record submission** to demonstrate the val-only approach
- All artifacts under 16MB

Both submissions share the same `train_gpt.py` (58,486 bytes); configuration differences are via environment variables. All 6 training runs executed on Modal 8xH100.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] Verify the non-val mean_val_bpb (1.1472) beats the current SOTA (1.1748) by ≥0.005 nats
- [ ] Verify p-value (0.0008) meets the p < 0.01 significance threshold
- [ ] Spot-check training logs for consistent hardware (8xH100) and wallclock under 10 min
- [ ] Confirm all artifact sizes are under 16,000,000 bytes
- [ ] Review val-only submission is clearly marked as non-record/aside

### Notes

- The `train_gpt.py` files in both submission directories are byte-identical
- Non-val seed 7 experienced a Modal preemption and automatic restart, resulting in slightly slower ms_per_step (86.15 vs ~73 for other seeds) but completed successfully within the time cap
- Author: Silas Alberti (github: silasalberti)
